# #14030 AC1 producer-core: supervised-task failure/overdue diagnosis helpers

## Context

#14030 AC1 (alert on backup failure/overdue) converged with @neo-gpt (https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/14030#issuecomment-4806173915): a **sibling task-diagnostics producer** (NOT bolted into `ContainerHealthDiagnosisService`, NOT a pageable recovery-actuator target) that emits a `recovery-diagnosis` event with `targetIdentity:{kind:'supervised-task', id}` + `details.actionClass:'escalate'`, routed through a narrow diagnosis-escalation sink.

## The Fix (this slice — the producer-core)

Pure functions in `ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/taskOutcomeDiagnosis.mjs`:
- `detectTaskOverdue({lastRunAt, intervalMs, graceMs, now})` → `{overdue, overdueByMs}` (interval<=0 disables).
- `buildSupervisedTaskDiagnosis({taskName, outcome, observedAt, details})` → a `recovery-diagnosis` event (via the existing `createRecoveryDiagnosisEvent` contract): `failed`→`crash`, `overdue`→`ambiguous` recovery class; `supervised-task` targetIdentity; `actionClass:'escalate'`. Never restarts/retries — detection only.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `detectTaskOverdue` returns the overdue verdict (past `lastRunAt+intervalMs+graceMs`); `<=0` interval never overdue.
- [ ] `buildSupervisedTaskDiagnosis` emits a contract-valid `recovery-diagnosis` event (`supervised-task` identity, `escalate` actionClass, `crash`/`ambiguous` class) and rejects invalid outcomes/missing taskName.
- [ ] Unit coverage for both.

## Out of Scope (the next AC1 slice — live wiring)

- The `ProcessSupervisorService.recordTaskOutcome('<task>','failed', …)` hook (line 412 etc.) calling the producer for the alert-on-failure task set.
- The scheduling-loop overdue check (`scheduling/backup.mjs` `lastRunAt`+interval) calling `detectTaskOverdue`.
- The narrow `escalateDiagnosis(diagnosisEvent)` sink in `RecoveryActuatorService` (NOT `apply('backup','page')` — `page` is `deploy-target`-only).

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Consumed surface | Field | Type / values | Change | Consumer impact | Fallback / semantics |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `detectTaskOverdue()` return (exported from `taskOutcomeDiagnosis.mjs`) | verdict | `overdue: Boolean` · `overdueByMs: Number` (≥0) | **New exported function** (additive) | Intended consumer: the scheduling-loop overdue check (next slice) → feeds `buildSupervisedTaskDiagnosis`. | Pure; `intervalMs <= 0` ⇒ `{overdue:false, overdueByMs:0}` (periodic disabled); absent `lastRunAt` treated as 0. |
| `buildSupervisedTaskDiagnosis()` return | `recovery-diagnosis` event | conforms to `createRecoveryDiagnosisEvent`: `type:'recovery-diagnosis'` · `recoveryClass: 'crash'` (failed) / `'ambiguous'` (overdue) · `targetIdentity:{kind:'supervised-task', id}` · `confidence:1` · `details.actionClass:'escalate'` + `details.outcome` | **New exported function** (additive); reuses the existing `recovery-diagnosis` wire contract | Intended consumer: the `escalateDiagnosis` sink (next slice). Producer-only — emits the event; never restarts/retries. | Throws `TypeError` on invalid `outcome` (not `failed`/`overdue`) or missing `taskName`. |

- **Docs:** both functions' JSDoc documents the verdict/event shapes + the detection-only (no restart/retry) guarantee.
- **Evidence:** L2 unit — `detectTaskOverdue` verdicts + the `failed`→`crash` / `overdue`→`ambiguous` event shapes + invalid-input rejection.

## Related

- #14030 — parent (AC1); #14039 — v13.1 epic. AC2 (#14053) / AC3 stay separate.
